title: 10 best herbal tinctures for wheezing
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for wheezing are concentrated plant extracts used to alleviate respiratory issues such as wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath. They work by reducing inflammation, opening airways, and soothing the respiratory tract. The best herbal tinctures for wheezing include Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Verbascum thapsus, Thymus vulgaris, Thymus serpyllum, Ephedra sinica, Hydrastis canadensis, Zingiber officinale, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, wheezing can significantly impact daily life, causing fatigue, difficulty sleeping, and decreased productivity, ultimately affecting mental and physical well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with wheezing because of its unique properties that work to reduce inflammation and calm respiratory issues. The tincture's active compounds, such as echinacoside and chlorogenic acid, have natural anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help to ease constriction in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms. Additionally, purple coneflower's antioxidant properties help to protect the lungs from further damage and oxidative stress, promoting overall respiratory health and relief from wheezing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate wheezing caused by conditions like bronchitis or asthma.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of purple coneflower, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, help combat infections that can contribute to wheezing, such as respiratory tract infections like pneumonia.

Expectorant properties: Purple coneflower's expectorant compounds, such as mucilages and saponins, help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing caused by conditions like chronic bronchitis or COPD.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce airway inflammation and alleviate wheezing symptoms.

Cichoric Acid: A phenolic compound found in Echinacea purpurea, cichoric acid has been demonstrated to exhibit anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which could help mitigate respiratory issues and wheezing.
Alkylamides: These compounds have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which may help relax airway muscles and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of compounds that are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties.
Flowers: They are used because they contain flavonoids and alkylamides that have been traditionally used to help with respiratory issues, including wheezing.
Leaves: They are used for their content of phenolic acids and caffeic acid, which are thought to have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature echinacea purpurea roots in the fall after first frost, drying them immediately to preserve potency.
Chop 1 part dried echinacea roots into smaller pieces and combine with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and allow the mixture to macerate for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in the refrigerator to preserve shelf life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with wheezing because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and relieve congestion. Glycyrrhizin, a key component of licorice root, has been shown to decrease mucus production and improve respiratory function, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, the expectorant properties of licorice tincture can help loosen and clear out phlegm from the lungs, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a saponin found in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles to alleviate wheezing symptoms.

Isoflavans: Isoflavans, a type of flavonoid present in licorice tinctures, have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and inflammation that may contribute to wheezing.

Saponins: Saponins, another class of compounds found in licorice root, have been traditionally used to soothe and calm respiratory tract mucous membranes, helping to reduce bronchial constriction and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins (Glycyrrhizin): These saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, easing wheezing symptoms.

Flavonoids (Isoliquiritigenin and Liquiritigenin): These flavonoids exhibit bronchodilatory effects, meaning they can help relax and widen airway passages, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing.

Terpenoids (Licoricidin and Licorisoflavone B): These terpenoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the airways from damage and reduce the severity of wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make tinctures for wheezing because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for wheezing because they are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that help to relax airways and reduce inflammation in the lungs.
Barks: They are used to make tinctures for wheezing because they contain saponins and other compounds that have expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 kg of fresh roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris that may be present.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife or a root cutter to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio by weight and allow it to steep for 2-3 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the tincture from the plant material and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tinctures helps with wheezing because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated airways. The thymol in thyme also exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear out mucus and congestion, allowing for easier breathing. Additionally, thyme's antiviral and antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections or inflammation that may be contributing to wheezing. By addressing these issues, herbal thyme tinctures can provide relief from wheezing and promote improved respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Thyme's antispasmodic compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to relax bronchial muscles and reduce spasms that can cause wheezing, making breathing easier.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and wheezing by allowing for easier passage of air in and out of the lungs.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds have been shown to combat bacterial infections that can contribute to wheezing, such as bronchitis or pneumonia, by inhibiting the growth of harmful bacteria and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol helps with wheezing by acting as a bronchodilator and reducing inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory properties, which help alleviate wheezing by relaxing airway muscles and reducing inflammation.
Rotundifolone: Rotundifolone has bronchodilatory effects, helping to open up the airways and relieve wheezing by reducing airway resistance and improving lung function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thymus vulgaris leaves are rich in thymol, a natural decongestant and expectorant that helps relieve wheezing by loosening mucus and reducing inflammation.
Flowers: The flowers of Thymus vulgaris contain thymol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate wheezing symptoms by reducing airway inflammation.
Stems: Thymus vulgaris stems contain a high concentration of thymol, which can help to relax airway muscles and improve breathing in individuals with wheezing conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in sufficient quantities for tincture preparation.
Combine 250 grams of thymus vulgaris with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar occasionally.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the thymus vulgaris tincture in a cool dark place and allow it to mature for 2 to 6 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein tinctures helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated airways. The herb's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out mucus, reducing congestion and making it easier to breathe. remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Mullein's mucilage helps soothe and lubricate irritated respiratory tissues, reducing inflammation and congestion that can contribute to wheezing.
Antioxidant activity: Mullein's antioxidant properties help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by pollutants, allergens, and other irritants, which can exacerbate wheezing.

remedy_constituents: Saponins: They help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, contributing to relief from wheezing associated with respiratory issues.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, which help to relax airway muscles and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
Flavonoids: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the airways, thereby reducing wheezing episodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for wheezing because they contain expectorant compounds that help relieve respiratory congestion.
Stems: The stems are used to make tinctures for wheezing because they contain mucilages that help soothe and calm irritated airways.
Roots: The roots are used to make tinctures for wheezing because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and congestion in the lungs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowers of the plant in the morning after the dew has evaporated about 2 cups.
Dry the flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours in a single layer.
Combine 2 cups of the dried flowers with 4 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks shaking the container every 2 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tinctures helps with wheezing because of its natural expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways. remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high levels of eucalyptol, a primary active compound responsible for the decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: They have a high concentration of eucalyptol and other volatile oils, making them effective in relieving respiratory issues.
Stems: They also contain eucalyptol, which contributes to their decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them useful in alleviating wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in early morning when the dew is still present on the leaves.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly by rinsing them with water and gently pat them dry with paper towels.
Combine 250 grams of the cleaned leaves with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tinctures helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm irritated airways. The plantain plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. When taken in a tincture form, the active compounds in plantain are quickly absorbed into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from wheezing symptoms by reducing inflammation and congestion in the lungs. This natural remedy can be especially helpful for individuals who experience frequent or severe wheezing episodes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Plantain's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the airways, which can contribute to wheezing, allowing for easier breathing.
Expectorant: The expectorant properties of Plantain help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the lungs, sinuses, and airways, making it easier to expel and reducing wheezing.
Antispasmodic: Plantain's antispasmodic properties relax smooth muscle in the airways, calming spasms and contractions that can cause wheezing, coughing, and difficulty breathing.
remedy_constituents: Apolipoprotein B-100-binding peptides: These peptides help with wheezing by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response in the airways.
Saponins: Saponins help by reducing inflammation and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to wheezing and respiratory issues.

Phenolic acids (such as rosmarinic acid): Phenolic acids, particularly rosmarinic acid, help by acting as antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain mucilages, which help soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing wheezing.
Seeds: Plantago seeds are often used in tinctures due to their expectorant properties, helping to relieve congestion and wheezing.
Stems: Stems from Plantago major are sometimes used in tinctures as they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and ease respiratory issues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried plantago major leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Combine the plantago major material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in the jar.
Seal the jar and allow the mixture to macerate for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the plantago major tincture in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties to help relieve wheezing symptoms.

Steep the root mixture in 80% ethanol for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain and filter the tincture, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to dark glass bottles for storage.

remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing respiratory issues like wheezing.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a natural expectorant that helps loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, providing relief from wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality foeniculum vulgare seeds from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Chop fresh or dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems into small pieces weighing 10 ounces.
Combine chopped foeniculum vulgare material with vodka at a ratio of 1:5 for 4 weeks soaking time.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the tincture.
Bottle the clear yellow liquid and store it in a cool dark place for up to one year before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with wheezing because its calming properties soothe the respiratory tract, relaxing bronchial muscles and reducing inflammation. The herb's natural antispasmodic effects also help to ease constriction of airways, allowing for smoother breathing and reduced wheezing symptoms. Additionally, lemon balm's antimicrobial properties may help to combat underlying infections that can exacerbate wheezing. By promoting relaxation and alleviating respiratory discomfort, herbal lemon balm tinctures provide natural relief from wheezing and related breathing issues.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lemon balm's antispasmodic properties help to relax and calm bronchial smooth muscle, reducing spasms and constrictions that can contribute to wheezing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the airways, which is often a contributing factor to wheezing and respiratory issues like asthma.
Expectorant properties: Lemon balm's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to ease respiratory issues and reduce inflammation, making them effective for treating wheezing.
Seeds: Containing oil-rich components with anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe the airways and alleviate wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality melissa officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning after dew.
Cleanly dry the herb material using paper towels or clean cloth sheets for several hours.
Chop the dried melissa officinalis into small pieces and fill a glass jar about 2/3 full.
Pour 80 proof vodka over the herb material, leaving about an inch at the top of the jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking daily to allow tincture to infuse.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Selfheal tinctures helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains flavonoids, which are known to reduce inflammation in the airways and bronchi, helping to alleviate symptoms such as coughing and shortness of breath. Additionally, selfheal has been shown to relax airway muscles, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing sounds. Its expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs, further relieving wheezing episodes.
remedy_properties: Antitussive and expectorant: Selfheal tincture's soothing and calming effects can help reduce coughing fits associated with wheezing, making it easier to breathe.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate congestion and swelling in the airways, which can contribute to wheezing symptoms.
Antioxidant: Selfheal's antioxidant compounds may help protect the lungs from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental irritants or infections, which can exacerbate wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of selfheal contain a high concentration of mucilages, which helps to soothe and calm irritated airways, relieving wheezing.
Barks: The barks of selfheal have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviating wheezing symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried prunella vulgaris leaves and flowers in amounts of one ounce per pint of menstruum.
Combine the harvested material with vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, leaving an inch of headspace for expansion.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a dark cool place for four to six weeks, shaking daily during this time period.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the tincture.
Transfer the final product to dark amber bottles and label them with date prepared, botanical name, and dosage instructions for adult use of 20-30 drops up to three times daily.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation in the airways, which can contribute to wheezing and other respiratory issues.
Anti-inflammatory: The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help alleviate swelling and constriction of the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing.
Bronchodilatory: Pot marigold contains flavonoids that can help relax smooth muscles in the airways, opening up passageways and improving airflow, which can reduce wheezing and coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax airway muscles, alleviating symptoms of wheezing.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain a high concentration of omega-3 fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues associated with wheezing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers at dawn when the dew is still present on the petals.
Chop the harvested flowers into smaller pieces and combine them with 50% vodka or brandy in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily, until the liquid reaches a dark yellow color.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the clear liquid.
Store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature for up to 2 years, shaking well before using as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Brahmi tinctures helps with wheezing because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the airways. The herbs in the tincture, such as Bacopa monnieri, work to reduce inflammation and irritation in the lungs, allowing for easier breathing and reduced congestion. This can be especially helpful for individuals who suffer from wheezing caused by conditions such as asthma or chronic bronchitis. Bronchodilatory effects: The bioactive compounds in Brahmi, such as saponins and flavonoids, can relax bronchial muscles and widen airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing wheezing symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Brahmi's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can cause swelling and constriction of airways, leading to wheezing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Brahmi plant are used to create a tincture that helps to soothe and calm respiratory issues, including wheezing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried bacopa monnieri leaves and stems for maximum potency and freshness.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to release the essential oils and flavonoids.
Combine the chopped bacopa monnieri with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a ratio of 1:2.
Store the mixture in a dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar twice daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer the resulting liquid to small amber glass bottles.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to wheezing that are treated with herbal tinctures are bronchitis, asthma, and chronic coughs. supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with wheezing is often found in a blend of Thyme, Eucalyptus, and Ginger. Thyme, rich in antimicrobial properties, helps combat underlying infections that may be contributing to wheezing. Eucalyptus, with its decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects, eases respiratory congestion and reduces inflammation in the airways. Ginger, known for its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, helps to break down mucus and promote a smooth cough, ultimately alleviating wheezing symptoms.
